snippet.title: Macbook PRO M4   1 year honest review
snippet.description:
MacBook Pro M4 (16-inch) — The Good, The Bad & The Honest Review

Once I made the switch to macOS, I never looked back. I started with a Mac Mini M1, and about a year ago, I upgraded to the MacBook Pro M4 16-inch — and this thing changed how I work.

In this video, I’m going in-depth about the good, the bad, and my honest experience after using it daily as a developer and content creator.

⸻

💎 The Good
 • Battery life is unreal — it lasts forever.
 • The display? The best my eyes have ever seen.
 • The speakers — wow. Best I’ve ever heard on a laptop.
 • Barely ever crashes.
 • Edits 4K video like it’s 1080p.

⸻

⚠️ The Bad

Not many, but they’re worth mentioning:
 • No native window manager (you’ll need third-party apps).
 • macOS gets expensive fast if you like nice utilities.
 • The Command key takes time to get used to.
 • Menu bar customization is limited — and apps like Ice broke with recent updates.
 • App updates aren’t seamless.
 • You need to grant permissions for almost everything.

⸻

💬 Honest Review

I’d only keep a Windows machine if I was gaming — since I’m not, I don’t look back. Whenever I use Windows again, I remember why I switched first to Linux and then to macOS. It’s simply better for developers.

💰 Is it worth the price?
For me — absolutely. I use it at least 4 hours daily, often up to 8. I got the 24GB / 500GB version, and that’s plenty. Large files live on my SSD, and performance has been rock solid.

snippet.title: Bad News for M5 MacBook Air?
snippet.description:
🔔 Subscribe for more Apple content, updates, and deep dives:
https://www.youtube.com/saranbyte/
👍 Like the video if you found it helpful - it really helps the channel grow!

Buy the M4 MacBook Air here (affiliate links)
UK - https://amzn.to/48PWHWo
US - https://amzn.to/3WpHeVy

Bad news for the M5 MacBook Air - or at least, that’s what some reviewers are saying. 😬 Vadim from Max Tech recently pointed out that the single fan inside the new M5 MacBook Pro isn’t keeping up under heavy workloads. In Cinebench 2024, the M5 chip showed signs of thermal throttling, with the single to multi-core ratio dropping from 5.38x on the M4 to 5.07x on the M5. ⚙️ Even though it’s faster overall, it can’t maintain peak performance during long, intensive use - leading Vadim to call out Apple directly: “Please give us two fans!” 🧊

Before you panic, this isn’t the disaster it sounds like. The MacBook Air isn’t made for sustained high-load tasks - it’s for everyday users who work in browsers, office apps, and creative tools with the occasional performance spike. 💻 Even with throttling, both the M5 base Pro and the upcoming M5 Air still outperform over 90% of Windows laptops in their class 🚀

So if you’ve seen those “bad news” headlines about heat and performance, don’t stress - this is a non-issue for most people. The M5 Air will still be cool, silent, and faster than ever for day-to-day use 🔋

Apple is now preparing the M5 MacBook Air (J813 & J815) for launch in early 2026, following the same spring refresh pattern as the M3 and M4 models. 🌸 Expect the same 13-inch and 15-inch sizes, the same sleek chassis, and a focus on internal upgrades — including the powerful 3nm M5 chip, up to 32GB unified memory, improved GPU and Neural Engine, and faster storage ⚡

While there’s no redesign yet, a major display upgrade is rumoured for 2027, using oxide TFT LCD tech for better power efficiency, thinner bezels, and possibly ProMotion for smoother visuals. 📺 OLED isn’t expected until later this decade, as Apple prioritises bringing that to the MacBook Pro lineup first

If you’re choosing between the M4 and M5 Air, the M4 remains an incredible value - often selling for around $799 - while the M5 will offer better AI and GPU performance for creators and students 🎓

💬 QUESTION FOR YOU:
Do you think the M5 MacBook Air will have thermal throttling issues? Let me know in the comments below 👇

snippet.title: Apple's M5 Mac Mini (2026): Full Redesign, 16-Core CPU, & Thunderbolt 5 LEAKS
snippet.description:
Apple's M5 Mac Mini is coming in 2026, and it's not an upgrade—it's a replacement. Leaks point to a silent, 2nm powerhouse designed to make the Mac Studio obsolete. This is Apple's secret weapon, and it's not what you think.

For years, the Mac Mini has been the humble, "entry-level" Mac. That's about to end. We are diving deep into the leaks and logical conclusions for the 2026 Apple M5 Mac Mini, a machine built not for iteration, but for total dominance.

This isn't just about a faster CPU. We're exploring the 2-nanometer M5 chip, a massive 16-core GPU with next-gen Ray Tracing that could finally make Mac Gaming a reality, and the true revolution: a rumored 128-core Neural Engine.

This is the on-device AI powerhouse that kills the cloud. Imagine a Siri that actually works, local large language models, and AI-powered creative workflows that don't need an internet connection.

But Apple isn't stopping there. We break down the full redesign, the new thermal system, the return of pro-level ports like Thunderbolt 5 and 10-Gig Ethernet, and the biggest twist of all: how the M5 Pro Mac Mini is poised to kill the Mac Studio line completely.

The era of the "Mini" is over. The era of the "Silent Killer" has just begun.

snippet.title: iPad Pro M5: Lo compré y te cuento TODO!
snippet.description:
Compré el iPad Pro M5 de 13" y le hice unboxing + primeras impresiones con comparativas rápidas frente al M4. ¿Vale realmente la pena el salto?
Spoiler: hay mejoras… pero no para todos. 👀

Qué verás en este video
• Unboxing completo del iPad Pro M5 (silver)
• Comparaciones físicas y compatibilidades (Magic Keyboard, Pencil Pro, USB-C/Thunderbolt)
• Novedades clave: M5 más potente, 12GB en base, Wi-Fi 7, carga rápida 50% en 35 min
• Qué NO cambió: cámaras, audio, Face ID, iPadOS sigue siendo el “techo”
• ¿Conviene pasar del M4 al M5? Mi recomendación honesta

Capítulos
00:00 Intro
00:25 Unboxing y accesorios incluidos
01:40 Diseño y primer vistazo (13", silver, 5.1 mm)
03:05 M5 vs M4: diferencias externas y detalle “sin iPad Pro”
04:30 Compatibilidad: Magic Keyboard y Apple Pencil Pro/USB-C
05:40 Especificaciones clave: CPU/GPU, Neural Engine, RAM y storage
07:30 Pantalla OLED ProMotion (Retina XDR) + nanotexture
09:10 Batería y nueva carga rápida (hasta 50% en 35 min)
10:20 Conectividad: Thunderbolt, Wi-Fi 7, Bluetooth 6
11:20 Lo que no cambió (cámaras, audio, Face ID, Siri)
12:20 ¿Actualizar o no desde M4? Mi veredicto
13:30 Próximos videos y despedida

Mi veredicto rápido
Si vienes de M4, no veo motivo fuerte para saltar a M5 (salvo casos muy puntuales de GPU + flujos pro). Si vienes de un iPad mucho más antiguo o no tienes iPad, el M5 es el tope de gama… pero también el más caro.

snippet.title: M5 iPad Pro: Did Apple just kill Samsung DeX?
snippet.description:
✨NEW✨ Paperlike V3! 👉🏼 https://geni.us/Paperlike251  
What was already great is even better now!

The M5 iPad Pro is here and here is my honest feedback so far, I hope you enjoy it 🙏🏼 I did this video with a 'buying guide' tone to help you if you are on the fence, or not sure the specs to get on this new M5 iPad Pro. 

As you will see today, there's a few considerations before pushing that 'buy now' button 😂 iPadOS 26 finally fixes the biggest pain points with Stage Manager and windowing, which in theory means your M2/M4 iPad Pro might be all you need.
